sector: Semiconductors — power (GaN/SiC) for AI data-center power delivery, grid, EV/industrial electrification
bull_thesis: Navitas is the cleanest small-cap pure-play on the ONE physical bottleneck every AI rack must pay through: power. NVIDIA's next-gen 800V HVDC architecture (Rubin/Kyber, 1MW+ racks) requires a wholesale shift from 54V to 800V DC distribution, and Navitas was named (2025-05-21, reconfirmed into the NVIDIA MGX/Kyber ecosystem 2026-06-03) as a power-semi partner using BOTH GaNFast (GaN) and GeneSiC (SiC) — a rare dual-technology capability only a handful of suppliers have. The 'Navitas 2.0' pivot is real and visible in the numbers: revenue +18% QoQ to $8.6M (above the high end of guide), non-GAAP GM expanding to 39.0%, data-center+grid +50% QoQ, high-power +35% YoY, with mobile/consumer deliberately abandoned (becomes 'insignificant' by year-end). Management sizes a $3.5B SAM by 2030 at 60%+ CAGR. The balance sheet is pristine — $221M cash, ZERO debt, capital-light fabless model (vs Wolfspeed's $4.6B debt stack and broken-yield capex) — so the company can fund the ramp to the 800V revenue inflection ('end of year, early next year') without a solvency wall. If 800V converts to sole/dual-source sockets in NVIDIA reference designs in H2-2026/2027, revenue can scale from ~$41M (2026E) toward ~$95M (2027E consensus) and beyond, and a content-per-rack design-in is a sticky, multi-year switching-cost moat. This is asymmetric optionality on the single highest-velocity capex wave in tech.
bear_thesis: You are paying ~$5.6B (~136x trailing / ~100x+ forward sales) for a company that today does ~$41M of SHRINKING revenue (−38% YoY), loses money on a GAAP gross basis (GAAP GM −9.3%; the 39% is non-GAAP only), posts a −$33.8M GAAP net loss per quarter, and needs ~4x current revenue just to reach break-even. The entire valuation is the NVIDIA-AI option, and that option ALREADY RAN — the stock printed an all-time high of $34.17 on 2026-06-03 (the MGX/Kyber announcement day) and has since round-tripped to ~$24. Worse, management is monetizing the pop, not buying it back: a $122M ATM was completed in May-2026 and a $500M ATM was launched 2026-06-13 (~9% potential dilution against the cap; stock −6.7% on the news). The 'strong balance sheet' is being topped up by selling stock into the narrative. The load-bearing 800V data-center revenue is still a 2027 promise ('end of year, early next year'), management answers volume questions with SAMPLE COUNTS and efficiency percentages rather than booked dollars or named customers, and NVIDIA can and will multi-source power semis (Infineon, onsemi, Power Integrations, EPC, TI all chase the same socket). If the ramp slips a quarter or NVIDIA dual-sources, there is NO fundamental support under the multiple — a 50%+ de-rate to a still-rich ~10-30x forward sales is the base-case downside. The thesis hinges on the SAME single shared macro factor (AI-capex durability) as the rest of Basket D — zero resolution-driver independence.
sop_bull: Whole-company intrinsic (no segment SoP block; one reporting line). Bull case sizes NVTS as a credible AI-power franchise: assume 800V converts and 2027 revenue reaches ~$95M (consensus) scaling to ~$250-300M by 2030 on the $3.5B SAM at design-in share. Put a generous ~15-18x EV/sales on a ~$250M 2030 revenue base (POWI trades ~10x on PROVEN profitability; NVTS gets a growth premium for hyper-scaler design-in), discounted back ~3 years at ~12% ≈ ~$3.0-3.4B EV → add $221M net cash, less ~$400-500M of cumulative ATM dilution proceeds already raised/used → equity ~$3.0-3.3B / ~255M fully-diluted shares ≈ $12-13 PV today... but bulls anchor to the FORWARD un-discounted 2027-2028 socket-win scenario where a $500M+ revenue run-rate at ~12x = ~$6B EV / ~$24-26/sh. Bull SoP per share: ~$28-34 (i.e. the recent ATH is the bull's fair value, requiring the socket win to convert on schedule). Parts: core AI-power design-in optionality ~$26-31/sh + net cash ~$0.9/sh + GaN/SiC IP/legacy ~$1-2/sh.
sop_bear: Whole-company intrinsic. Bear strips the un-booked optionality and values the demonstrated business: ~$41M 2026E revenue, still loss-making, at a generous-but-sane ~12-15x forward sales (a premium to profitable POWI's ~10x, crediting the NVIDIA halo and net cash) on a ~$55-65M 2027 revenue base if the ramp is real but modest ≈ ~$0.7-1.0B EV. Add $221M net cash, fully-diluted ~255M shares (post both ATMs). Equity ~$0.9-1.2B → ~$3.5-4.7/sh. If the 800V socket disappoints / NVIDIA multi-sources and the multiple compresses toward the loss-making-cohort (Wolfspeed ~4x sales), EV ~$220-320M + cash → ~$2-2.5/sh. Bear SoP per share: ~$3.5-5 (an ~80% drawdown from $24). Parts: demonstrated GaN/SiC revenue at peer multiple ~$2.6-4/sh + net cash ~$0.9/sh, with the AI optionality marked to ~zero.
sop_breakdown: Base case blends a real-but-unproven 800V ramp with heavy execution/dilution haircuts. Demonstrated + near-term high-power business: ~$55-75M 2027 revenue at ~14-16x forward sales (premium to profitable POWI ~10x for the NVIDIA design-in, discount to the bull's socket-win scenario) ≈ ~$0.9-1.2B EV → ~$3.5-4.7/sh. AI-capex/800V socket OPTIONALITY (probability-weighted, ~35-40% the socket converts to material multi-year content): ~$5-7/sh. Net cash $221M ≈ ~$0.9/sh (but eroding — being deployed into opex losses and offset by ATM dilution, so treated near-neutral). Less dilution overhang from the open $500M ATM (~9%): ~−$1/sh. Sum ≈ $10-13/sh fully-diluted (~255M sh). This sits ~45-55% BELOW the ~$24 market price — i.e. the market is pricing close to the bull's full socket-win, leaving negative risk-adjusted skew.
risk_reward: Unfavorable. Upside-to-fair-value: roughly −45% (fair $10-13 vs ~$24 price — the stock trades ABOVE base-case fair value). Downside-to-break (bear $3.5-5): −80%. Even the bull case (~$28-34) is only ~+20-40% AND requires the 800V socket to convert on schedule with no multi-sourcing. So the asymmetry is inverted: ~+30% best-case vs −80% bear-case, with base-case already below spot. Risk/reward ≈ 0.4:1 against.
peer_comps_note: True peers within the scale-out PWR-SEMI layer (GaN/SiC power for AI data-center power delivery), live multiples verified 2026-06 (web): Power Integrations (POWI) ~$4.84B cap, ~$475M FY26E rev → ~10x sales, PROFITABLE (fwd P/E ~38), the closest pure-play GaN/power-IC comp; onsemi ~$42.6B cap, ~$6.06B TTM rev → ~7x sales, profitable SiC scale leader; Infineon (IFX) ~$120B cap, ~4.6x EV/rev, profitable global scale/manufacturing leader; Wolfspeed (WOLF) ~$3.0B cap, ~$758M TTM rev → ~4x sales, post-restructuring, deeply loss-making (−$1.6B TTM net), $4.6B debt. NVTS ranks DEAD LAST on valuation discipline and FIRST on AI-capex narrative: ~$5.6B cap on ~$41M FY26E revenue = ~136x trailing / ~100x+ forward sales — roughly 10-30x the EV/sales multiple of profitable peers, on the SMALLEST and only YoY-SHRINKING revenue base in the cohort. On growth (sequential) it screens well; on margins (GAAP-gross-negative) and returns (deeply negative ROIC) it is worst-in-class among the non-distressed names. NVTS wins on balance sheet (zero debt vs Wolfspeed's $4.6B) and on the dual-GaN+SiC + NVIDIA-design-in narrative — but the entire ~$5.6B cap is that narrative; the demonstrated business would be worth a small fraction at peer multiples.
role_in_scaleout: Navitas supplies the GaN+SiC power semiconductors that enable NVIDIA's 800V HVDC rack architecture (Rubin/Kyber), sitting at the electrical-distribution / 800V-DC power conversion bottleneck inside the AI rack — a genuinely critical, physics-mandated layer; but its specific demand is NOT yet durable: it rests on an un-booked, multi-sourceable design-in slot whose material revenue is a 2027 promise, not a contracted backlog.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT looking the wrong way in the value-investor's direction — it is looking the wrong way in the momentum direction: consensus underwrites the 800V/NVIDIA socket converting cleanly to a $95M (2027E) → multi-hundred-million revenue ramp and capitalizes it at ~100x+ forward sales, treating a named-but-un-booked design-in as if it were contracted multi-year content. The factually wrong assumptions: (1) that the NVIDIA collaboration is exclusive/sole-source — it is a collaboration; NVIDIA explicitly architects MGX as a multi-vendor ecosystem and will dual-source power semis (Infineon, onsemi, POWI, EPC, TI all chase the identical socket); (2) that the 39% gross margin is the real margin — it is non-GAAP; GAAP gross margin is NEGATIVE (−9.3%) on under-absorbed volume; (3) that the $221M cash is a clean runway — management is simultaneously running a $500M ATM, so the 'fortress balance sheet' is being financed by, and diluted through, equity issuance into the narrative. The mispricing is that ~100% of the AI optionality is in the price with none of the execution risk discounted.
valley_of_death: Next 3-9 months: (1) DILUTION — the $500M ATM launched 2026-06-13 is an open overhang; every up-move invites issuance (~9% potential dilution vs the cap), capping the stock mechanically and the −6.7% reaction already showed the reflex. (2) NO catalyst left to fire — the load-bearing NVIDIA-MGX/Kyber news already hit (2026-06-03) and the stock round-tripped $34→$24; the next 800V revenue print is not expected until 'end of year / early next year', so there is a multi-quarter catalyst VACUUM during which the only news flow is more small-revenue prints (Q2 guided ~$10M) and more ATM sales. (3) Continued cash burn — non-GAAP op loss ~−$11.7M/qtr, GAAP net loss −$33.8M/qtr, FY2026 revenue (~$41M) far below the ~high-30s-millions-per-quarter needed for break-even, so losses persist all year. (4) Macro — the entire thesis rides AI-capex durability; any hyperscaler capex guide-down (the regime doc's named tripwire, ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth) de-grosses this name first and hardest as a high-beta, high-multiple, profitless proxy.
positioning_washout: This is a momentum/retail-and-thematic-fund crowd, not a yield or value base — there is no dividend to cut and no passive-income holder to force out. The washout risk runs the OTHER way: the holder base is fast money that bought the NVIDIA-AI story near $34 and is now ~30% underwater; a single disappointing 800V update, an AI-capex scare, or simply continued ATM dilution can trigger a momentum-unwind cascade with no valuation floor (the next support is the demonstrated-business value ~10-30x lower). The $500M ATM itself is a structural seller. There is no mechanical forced-buyer offset (the name is too speculative/loss-making for quality-factor or index inclusion at scale). Net: the positioning skew is a downside accelerant, not a coiled-spring upside washout.
forcing_function: There is no near-term BULLISH forcing function — the re-rate catalyst (NVIDIA-MGX/Kyber selection) already fired on 2026-06-03. The next hard dated events are BEARISH-to-neutral: Q2-FY2026 earnings (~early Aug-2026, ~$10M revenue, still deeply loss-making) and ongoing $500M-ATM share sales. The genuine bull forcing function — a FIRST material 800V data-center revenue print or a named, contracted hyperscaler/OEM design-win with dollar content — is management-guided to 'end of year, early next year' (Q4-FY2026/Q1-FY2027), i.e. 6-9+ months out and unverifiable today. Until that print, the consensus cannot be shattered upward, only eroded.
moderator_conclusion: PASS (C), conviction 2. Applying the current regime (complacent melt-up over a live AI-capex tail; the regime doc explicitly flags AI-capex as the dominant shared factor and mandates resolution-driver INDEPENDENCE at the book level): NVTS fails the independence test outright — its entire thesis is the SAME single AI-capex bet as the rest of Basket D, offering zero diversification and maximum correlation to the one factor that could de-gross the whole book. The load-bearing catalyst (NVIDIA 800V/MGX/Kyber selection) is FIRED — it printed the ATH on 2026-06-03 and the stock has already round-tripped, exactly the 'already-ran / out-of-window' setup the regime says to PENALIZE. What remains is a multi-quarter catalyst vacuum financed by a $500M ATM, against a ~136x-sales valuation with GAAP-gross-negative margins and persistent losses. The peer-comp sanity check is damning: every profitable peer (POWI ~10x, onsemi ~7x, Infineon ~4.6x sales) trades at a fraction of NVTS's multiple on far larger, profitable, growing revenue. Reconciled SoP fair value ~$10-13 sits ~45-55% BELOW the ~$24 price — the market is paying close to the bull's full socket-win scenario with none of the execution/dilution/multi-sourcing risk discounted. Risk/reward is inverted (~+30% bull-best vs −80% bear-break, base-case already below spot). The franchise is REAL and the secular wind is at its back (no terminal threat — this is a winner of electrification, not a value trap), and the dual-GaN+SiC + NVIDIA design-in is a genuine narrow moat in the making — but as a CAPITAL ALLOCATION at $24 it is a pass: you are buying fired optionality at a melting-cash, ATM-diluted, worst-in-cohort valuation. Would re-engage only on a deep washout toward the $8-12 SoP fair-value band AND a verified, contracted, dollar-denominated 800V design-win — i.e. a WATCHLIST-for-capitulation name, but only after a much larger flush than the current −30%-from-ATH. value_conviction 1: stripping the catalyst overlay entirely, the valuation versus the SoP fair value is the most stretched in the cohort and the demonstrated business loses money on a GAAP-gross basis — there is no value case here at all, only a richly-priced option.
interrogator_dossier:
# Interrogator Forensic Dossier — Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS)

*Prepared for the investment committee · scale-out Basket D (PWR-SEMI) · data as of 2026-06-22. No transcript bundle was provided; this dossier is built from the live Q1-FY2026 earnings release + call transcript (reported 2026-05-06), the NVIDIA collaboration disclosures (2025-05-21 and 2026-06-03), and the May/June-2026 ATM equity filings, all retrieved via web. Where a multi-quarter narrative arc is asserted it is sourced from the single live transcript plus dated press releases — not from a bundled 8-quarter set, and that limitation is flagged.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC
Navitas has executed a hard, deliberate pivot. The legacy story (through 2024) was a pure-play GaN fast-charging supplier levered to mobile phones, laptops and consumer chargers — a commoditizing, China-exposed, price-deflating end market. That base is now in structural run-off: Q1-FY2026 revenue of $8.6M was down ~38% YoY (from ~$14.0M in Q1-FY2025) precisely because management is walking away from low-end mobile/consumer. The current story is 'Navitas 2.0' — a re-platforming around high-power applications: AI data-center power delivery, grid/solid-state transformers, EV/industrial electrification. The marquee narrative pillar is the NVIDIA 800V HVDC collaboration: Navitas was named (2025-05-21) as a partner on NVIDIA's next-gen 800V high-voltage DC data-center architecture, and on 2026-06-03 was named into the NVIDIA MGX / 'Kyber' rack-scale ecosystem powering Rubin-Ultra-class GPUs, using both GaNFast (GaN) and GeneSiC (SiC). New CEO Chris Allexandre frames Q1 as 'the first clear proof point' of the pivot. Growth drivers introduced: 800V-to-6V DC-DC power-delivery boards, 1.2kV Gen-5 SiC, 250kW solid-state transformer. Driver quietly dropped: mobile, which becomes 'insignificant' by year-end. Management sizes the prize at a $3.5B SAM by 2030 at 60%+ CAGR. Credible strategic logic — but the arc is a promise, not yet a P&L; the 800V data-center revenue is explicitly back-half-2026/early-2027.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)
Revenue $8.6M (+18% QoQ but −38% YoY) — sequential momentum real, but YoY the business is shrinking as mobile rolls off. High-power +35% YoY / data-center+grid +50% QoQ — direction right, magnitude immaterial to a $5.6B cap. Non-GAAP GM 39.0% (+30bps) BUT GAAP GM −9.3% (negative) — the headline 39% omits under-absorbed fixed cost. $221M cash, zero debt — true, the one unambiguously strong fact. Path to profitability needs ~high-30s-millions revenue per quarter; non-GAAP op loss −$11.7M, GAAP net loss −$33.8M — break-even needs ~4x current revenue; FY2026 guide only ~$41M → loss-making all year. Capital allocation: $122M ATM completed May-2026 + $500M ATM launched 2026-06-13 — the 'strong balance sheet' is funded by selling stock into the AI pop. Decisive divergence: a ~136x trailing-sales valuation underwritten by revenue that is down YoY, GAAP-gross-margin-negative, and cash-flow-negative.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY
Rising / offensive — but on design-win optionality, not delivered results. New CEO promotional ('when you get to 5,000 samples, it's not samples'); heavy efficiency superlatives (98%+, 97.5% peak, 50% power density). Classic pre-revenue ramp framing. Defensive where it counts: declines to quantify design-win revenue, customer names, or concentration. Single-quarter view caps the trajectory read — flagged.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY
Limited track record in provided materials. Q1 revenue beat its own guide ($8.6M above high end); Q2 guided ~$10.0M (+16% QoQ) — being met/modestly beaten, but on a de-risked, shrunken base after the mobile reset (easy to clear). Reliability Medium — credible on near-term small steps; unproven on the load-bearing 800V revenue claim.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS
Recurring deflection: the bridge from samples to dollars — answers volume questions with sample counts and efficiency specs, not booked revenue, ship dates, or named customers. Second unaddressed worry: dilution — $500M ATM vs ~$5.6B cap, launched days after the June-3 pop (stock −6.7% on 2026-06-13; ~$24 vs $34 ATH set on the announcement day). Third: timing risk on 800V ('end of year, early next year') that could slip.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS
🔴 Revenue −38% YoY; FY2026 ~$41M can't fund the business → losses all year (Q1 GAAP net loss −$33.8M). 🔴 GAAP gross margin negative (−9.3%). 🔴 $500M ATM (2026-06-13) on top of $122M May ATM — serial equity issuance into the narrative; ~233.7M shares and rising. 🔴 ~136x trailing / ~100x+ forward sales — AI optionality fully priced; already round-tripped $34→$24. 🔴 Catalyst concentration on a single shared macro factor (AI-capex). 🟢 $221M cash, zero debt — genuine runway. 🟢 Real, named, repeatedly-reconfirmed NVIDIA design-in (2025-05-21 → 2026-06-03). 🟢 Dual GaN+SiC, capital-light fabless vs Wolfspeed's broken capex. 🟢 Sequential revenue + non-GAAP margin both inflecting up.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS
Terminology shift 'fast charging' → 'power delivery / HVDC / solid-state transformer' is the whole thesis in microcosm. Tell to watch: progress measured in sample counts and efficiency percentages — vocabulary of a company whose wins haven't converted to revenue. New-CEO voice materially more capital-markets-savvy/promotional. The $500M ATM is the loudest hidden-in-plain-sight signal: they are selling the optionality, not buying it back.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)
Trajectory: PIVOTING. Mid-transformation from a melting consumer-GaN base to an unproven-but-real AI-infrastructure-power franchise. Single most important thing to weigh: you are paying a ~$5.6B (≈136x sales) AI-infrastructure valuation for a company doing ~$40M of shrinking revenue, GAAP-gross-margin-negative, funded by a $500M ATM — essentially ALL of the AI-capex optionality is already in the price and the load-bearing 800V revenue is a 2027 promise. Cash prevents bankruptcy but not a 50%+ de-rate. Moat: NARROW — real but thin, intangible/design-in (dual GaN+SiC IP, NVIDIA reference-design slot, capital-light fabless); not scale-cost, not yet switching-cost (design-ins not in volume production); GAAP-gross-negative and deeply negative ROIC = no demonstrated return-based moat today, the moat is prospective. MOAT_TREND: STABLE (potentially widening if 800V converts; not yet evidenced). Secular: on the winning side of electrification/AI-power — no terminal-decline threat; legacy mobile is being abandoned, not value-trapped. Risk is cyclical/competitive and valuation, not terminal erosion (GaN/SiC contested by Infineon, onsemi, POWI, EPC, TI; hyperscaler can multi-source). SECULAR_THREAT: manageable. This is the opposite of cheap — a richly-priced growth-optionality name, not a fear-print.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 3 | TRAJECTORY: PIVOTING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: The load-bearing scale-out claim — a durable, defensible NVIDIA 800V design-in worth a ~$5.6B / ~100x+-forward-sales valuation — breaks on two independently verified primary facts. (1) MULTI-SOURCE, NOT SOLE-SOURCE: NVIDIA's 800V HVDC architecture is an explicit multi-vendor MGX collaboration; Infineon is named alongside Navitas working with NVIDIA on the IDENTICAL HVDC silicon/SiC/GaN socket (GlobeNewswire 2025-05-21; barchart/Yahoo MGX coverage 2026-06). It is a 'collaboration,' not a contracted, dollar-denominated, sole-source design-win — so the supposed switching-cost moat and content-per-rack durability are unproven and commoditizable. (2) FIRED OPTIONALITY FINANCED BY DILUTION: a $500M ATM was launched 2026-06-13 (UBS/Morgan Stanley/Needham, ~19.9M shares at the $25.08 pricing reference, ~3% commission) on top of a COMPLETED $122M May-2026 ATM (6,529,666 shares) — i.e. the 'fortress balance sheet' is being sold into the AI pop, with $221M cash already eroding QoQ from $237M (~$16M/qtr burn). Against this, the demonstrated business is GAAP-gross-NEGATIVE (-9.3% GM), Q1 revenue $8.6M is DOWN ~39% YoY, GAAP net loss is $33.8M/qtr, and FY2026 revenue (~$41M) is far below break-even — with the load-bearing 800V revenue still a 'end-of-year / early-next-year' (2027) promise. As a SCALE-OUT CONVICTION name the thesis fails: ~100% of the AI optionality is already in the price (ATH $34.17 on the 2026-06-03 announce day, round-tripped to ~$24), the catalyst is FIRED with a multi-quarter vacuum ahead, the demand is multi-sourceable and un-booked, and there is no fundamental support under the multiple. Default REFUTED stands — the bear independently confirmed every kill_fact against primary sources.
skeptic_corrections: None of the bear's load-bearing figures required correction — all verified to the primary record: Q1-FY2026 revenue $8.6M (-39% YoY vs $14.0M), GAAP GM -9.3% (non-GAAP 39.0%), GAAP net loss $33.8M, $221M cash (down from $237M QoQ) / zero debt, $122M May ATM completed (6.53M sh) + $500M ATM launched 2026-06-13 (~9% potential dilution). Two minor framing notes: (a) the $500M ATM agents are UBS / Morgan Stanley / Needham (the May $122M ATM agents were Craig-Hallum / UBS) — the debate left the agent set unstated; (b) diluted share count is ~230M post-May-ATM (the debate's ~233-255M 'fully diluted post-both-ATMs' is forward-looking and contingent on the open $500M ATM being drawn — directionally correct, not yet realized).
skeptic_evidence: GlobeNewswire, 'NVIDIA Selects Navitas to Collaborate on Next Generation 800 V HVDC Architecture,' 2025-05-21 — establishes the relationship as a collaboration on a reference architecture, not a sole-source supply contract (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/05/21/3086170/0/en/nvidia-selects-navitas-to-collaborate-on-next-generation-800-v-hvdc-architecture.html).

Barchart / Yahoo Finance, 'Navitas Collaborates with NVIDIA MGX Ecosystem to Accelerate 800 VDC AI Infrastructure,' 2026-06 — confirms MGX is a multi-vendor ecosystem and that Infineon is also working with NVIDIA on the same 800V HVDC silicon/SiC/GaN socket; Navitas attended the NVIDIA Partner Ceremony 2026-05-29 (Taipei).

StockTitan / MarketScreener SEC-filing coverage of Navitas Form S-3ASR + 424B5 prospectus supplement, 'Navitas launches $500M ATM for Class A shares,' filed 2026-06-13 — agents UBS / Morgan Stanley / Needham, up to 3.0% commission, $25.08 reference price (06-05) implying ~19,936,204 shares; stock -6.7% on the news; director Ranbir Singh resigned same day.

Navitas Q1-FY2026 results (reported 2026-05-06; Form 10-Q period ended 2026-03-31) via StockTitan / Compound Semiconductor — revenue $8.6M (-39% YoY vs $14.0M, +18% QoQ), GAAP gross margin -9.3% (non-GAAP 39.0%), GAAP net loss $33.8M (-$0.15 EPS), cash $221M (down from $237M QoQ), zero debt; plus the completed $122M May-2026 ATM (8-K, 6,529,666 shares).

scale.role: GaN/SiC hopeful claiming the NVDA 800V socket — but multi-sourced alongside IFNNY, revenue shrinking, serially diluting.
scale.rationale: Skeptic REFUTED: the sole-source 800V claim is FALSE (explicit multi-vendor MGX collaboration, IFNNY named on the identical socket); Q1 revenue -39% YoY, $500M ATM (~9-19% dilution), ~100x+ forward sales. REFUTED forces TACTICAL/PASS; with revenue shrinking and base $11.50 ~52% below a $24 spot I take PASS. Cap 8 honored.
scale.demand_durability: Low — revenue declining, no sole-source lock, micro-cap with a diluting balance sheet.
